Skip to contents
SpaMTP 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

Package index

All functions

AlignSpatialOmics()
Shiny app allowing for manual alignment of SM and ST data coordinates
AnnotateSM()
Annotates m/z values based on reference metabolite dataset
BinMetabolites()
Sums the intensity values of multiple m/z values into one
CardinalToSeurat()
Converts a Cardinal Object into a Seurat Object
Chebi_db
Chebi_db: Cleaned ChEBI (Chemical entities of biological interest) reference dataset
CheckAlignment()
Checks the alignment of two spatial datasets by plotting their relative coordinates on the same graph
ConvertSeuratToCardinal()
Converts a Seurat object to a Cardinal object, including annotations and metadata
DEPsHeatmap()
Generates a Heatmap of DEPs generated from edgeR analysis run using FindAllDEPs(). - this function uses pheatmap() to plot data
FindAllDEPs()
Finds all differentially expressed m/z values between comparison groups specified - This function uses run_pooling() and run_DE() to pool and run EdgeR analysis
FindCorrelatedFeatures()
Find top features and metabolites that are strongly correlated with a given feature
FindDuplicateAnnotations()
Finds if any metabolite is duplicated across multiple m/z values.
FindNearestMZ()
Finds the nearest m/z peak to a given value in the specified Seurat Object
FindSpatiallyVariableMetabolites()
Finds metabolites that display strong spatial patterns using MoransI
FisherexactTest()
Calculates Significant Metabolic Pathways using a Fisher Exact Test
GNPS_db
GNPS_db: A cleaned database of metabolites from GNPS
GetSpatiallyVariableMetabolites()
Gets the top n number of spatially variable features
HMDB_db
HMDB_db: A cleaned version of the reference metabolomics dataset from the Human Metabolome Database (HMDB)
ImageMZAnnotationPlot()
Visualise expression of metabolites in a spatial context - This is for plotting Seurat Spatial Metabolomic data without an image(i.e. H&E image) that has been annotated using annotate.SeuratMALDI() - This function inherits off Seurat::ImageFeaturePlot(). Look here for more detailed documentation about inputs.
ImageMZPlot()
Visualise expression of m/z values in a spatial context - This is for plotting Seurat Spatial Metabolomic data without an image(i.e. H&E image) - This function inherits off Seurat::ImageFeaturePlot(). Look here for more detailed documentation about inputs.
Lipidmaps_db
Lipidmaps_db: A cleaned version of the lipid database from LIPID MAPS
MZBoxPlot()
Generates a Boxplot of Spatial Metabolomic intensity data
MZRidgePlot()
Generates a ridge plot of Spatial Metabolomic intensity data
MZVlnPlot()
Generates a Violin plot of Spatial Metabolomic intensity data
MapSpatialOmics()
Converts and aggregates Spatial Metabolomic (MALDI) data to corresponding Spatial Transcriptomics (Visium) spots.

  • This function uses generate_new_SM_counts() and increase_SM_res()

MassIntensityPlot()
Plots mean mass spectrometry intensity values for a given Seurat Object, and groups by categories if supplied.
MultiOmicIntegration()
Multi-Omic integration of Spatial Metabolomics and Transcriptomics data using Seurat's Weighted Nearest Neighbours function
NormalizeSMData()
Normalizes m/z intensity data stored in a Seurat Object
Plot3DFeature()
Generates a 3D spatial feature plot from a SpaMTP object
ReadSM_mtx()
Read a Spatial Metabolomics image matrix file NOTE: This file must be in a format similar to the one below:
RefineLipids()
Uses common lipid nomenclature to simplify lipid annotations
ResizeMat()
Helper function to resize a matrix back to its original layout after sampling
RowVar()
Compute the row variances for each m/z value
SearchAnnotations()
Searches through annotated m/z values to return all which contain the metabolite search term provided
SpatialMZAnnotationPlot()
Visualise expression of metabolites in a spatial context for Spatial Seurat Objects with H&E images. - This is for plotting Seurat Spatial Metabolomic data without an image(i.e. H&E image) - This function inherits off Seurat::ImageFeaturePlot(). Look here for more detailed documentation about inputs.
SpatialMZPlot()
Visualise expression of m/z values in a spatial context for Spatial Seurat Objects with H&E images. - This is for plotting Seurat Spatial Metabolomic data without an image(i.e. H&E image) - This function inherits off Seurat::SpatialFeaturePlot(). Look here for more detailed documentation about inputs.
SubsetMZFeatures()
Subset a Seurat Spatial Metabolomic object by list of m/z's
TMMNormalize()
Performs TMM normalization between categories based on a specified ident
add_backslashes_to_specialfeatures()
Adds in backslashes required to take into account special using grepl such as brackets and +
add_ssc_annotation()
Adds Cardinal ssc segmentation annotation to m/z count data object

  • When ssc is run an object is returned which cannot plot raw m/z values

adduct_file
adduct_file: A dataframe containing possible adducts used for pathway analysis
analyte
analyte: A dataframe containing ID's of possible RAMP analytes
analytehaspathway
analytehaspathway: A dataframe containing RAMP_pathway ID's
bin.mz()
Bins multiple m/z values into one.
calculate_bounds()
Calculates the mz range of the observed_df
check_and_truncate_adduct_vector()
Checks if the complete adduct is in the data base, else returns a truncated adduct
check_column_type()
Helper function to determine if a column contains categorical or continuous Data
chem_props
chem_props: A database containing the chemical properties and metadata of each RAMP_DB analyte
combine.tr()
Combines rigid tranformation matrices
db_adduct_filter()
Filters the provided metabolomic database by polarity and adducts
find_index()
Finds the index values of the m/z values with their respective GSEA result
formula_filter()
Filters reference Database to only select natural elements
generate_new_SM_counts()
Generate new MALDI counts matrix for equivalent Visium spots

  • This function takes an original Spatial Metabolomics counts matrix, along with a metadata table (obs_x) containing information about the correspondence between MALDI and Visium spots. It generates a new counts matrix where each MALDI spot has an associated aggregated count based on its corresponding Visium spots.

getRefinedAnnotations()
Used to subset dataset to only include annotations that have n number of entries

  • (i.e. some peaks can have multiple annotations. Peaks which have above n number of annotations assigned will be removed from Seurat Object)

get_analytes_db()
Helper function for building a pathway db based on detected metabolites
get_optimal_layout()
Gets the optimal layout coordinates based on the number of different groups being plotted
getdensitymap()
Generates a 3D density plot for specific m/z values
increase_SM_res()
Increase the resolution of Spatial Metabolomics data for merging with Spatial Transcriptomic (Visium) data

  • This function takes a Spatial Metabolomics dataset and increases its resolution by generating pseudo-high-resolution spots. This is useful for merging the data with Spatial Transcriptomic (Visium) data that has a different resolution.

  • This function uses the split_pixel() function

is_formula_valid()
Checks if a formula contains only the allowed elements
labels_to_show()
Filters the annotation list to only include the first n number of annotations per m/z
list_to_pprcomp()
Creates a pprcomp object based on an input list
loadSM()
Loads in spatial metabolomic data directly to a SpaMTP Seurat Object
mirror()
Creates a transformation matrix that mirrors an object in 2D along either the x axis or y axis around its center of mass
pathway
pathway: A dataframe containing RAMP_DB pathways and their relative metadata
pathway_analysis()
This the function used to compute the exact fisher test for over-representation based pathway analysis
pixelPlot()
Helper function for converting Seurat Class ggplots from spot to pixel layout
plot_plus_minus()
Helper Function to generate merged counts within the plus minus range provided
plusminus()
Identifies all mz peaks within a plus-minus range of the target_mz

  • This function uses FindNearestMZ()

ppm_error()
Calculates the ppm error as a valve
ppm_range_match()
Calculates the ppm range and check if mz values are within the range

  • Returns TRUE if match is found and false if no match.

principal_component_pathway_analysis()
PCA driven pathway analaysis
proc_db()
Searches observed mz values against the data base list and returns matching annotations
rescale()
Helper function to rescale a sampled matrix
rigid.refl()
Creates a transformation matrix for reflection
rigid.rot()
Creates a transformation matrix for rotation
rigid.stretch()
Creates a transformation matrix for stretching
rigid.transf()
Creates a transformation matrix for rotation and translation
rigid.transl()
Creates a transformation matrix for translation with an offset of (h, k)
run_DE()
Runs EdgeR analysis for pooled data - This function is used by run_edgeR_annotations()
run_pooling()
Runs pooling of a merged Seurat Dataset to generate pseudo-replicates for each sample - This function is used by run_edgeR_annotations()
saveSeuratData()
Saves Seurat Spatial Metabolomic object to files.

  • This can be used for saving data for transfer to python Anndata using scanpy.read_10x_mtx()

  • For saving in R saveRDS() is recommended

save_pheatmap_as_pdf()
Saves the DEPs generated pheatmap as a PDF
source_df
source_df: A dataframe containing source information about RAMP_ID analyte used for analysis
split_pixel()
Splits a pixel into multiple spots - This function takes a central point and a pixel radius and splits the pixel into multiple smaller spots based on the specified configuration.
statPlot()
Helper function for QC plots by generating intensity count data
stretch()
Stretch along angle
subset_SPM()
Intermediate solution to subset(): subset FOVs/centroids if selected cells are NOT found in each FOV NOTE: some code parts and args are taken from SeuratObject Function params/args:
translate()
Creates a transformation matrix that translates an object in 2D
verbose_message()
Helper function for suppressing function progress messages

Developed by Andrew Causer.

Site built with pkgdown 2.1.0.
